description.en: The `/etc/group-` file contains a backup list of all the valid groups defined in the system.

rationale.en: It is critical to ensure that the `/etc/group-` file is protected from unauthorized access. Although it is protected by default, the file permissions could be changed either inadvertently or through malicious actions.

audit.en:
Run the following commands to remove excess permissions, set owner, and set group on `/etc/group-`:

```
# chmod u-x,go-wx /etc/group-
# chown root:root /etc/group-
```

remediation.en:
Run the following commands to remove excess permissions, set owner, and set group on `/etc/group-`:

```
# chmod u-x,go-wx /etc/group-
# chown root:root /etc/group-
```
